title: SSO login fails with SAML provider
description:
When users attempt to log in via SAML SSO, the OAuth callback handler throws a 500 error.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to /login
2. Click 'Sign in with SSO'
3. Complete SAML authentication
4. Observe 500 error on callback

## Expected
User should be redirected to dashboard after successful SAML auth.

## Actual
500 Internal Server Error on `/auth/saml/callback`.

notes.body: I can reproduce this consistently. The issue is in the OAuth callback handler — it doesn't handle the SAML assertion correctly when the IdP returns multiple group memberships.
Confirmed. The `parse_saml_response` function needs to handle the `memberOf` attribute as an array, not a string.

merge_requests.title: Fix SAML callback handler for multi-group responses

title: Add rate limiting to API endpoints
description:
Implement rate limiting on all public API endpoints to prevent abuse.

## Requirements
- 100 requests/minute for authenticated users
- 20 requests/minute for unauthenticated users
- Return 429 with Retry-After header when limit exceeded
- Use Redis for distributed rate counting
